description_long: This object is a straight-edged sword with a broad, flat blade that tapers to a point. The blade appears metallic and has a matte, silvery finish, suggesting it is made of steel or a similar metal. The handle is cylindrical and dark, likely constructed from metal or a dense synthetic material, and ends in a simple, faceted pommel. A flat, rectangular crossguard separates the blade from the handle and is colored gold or brass, providing both functional hand protection and a visual accent. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible engravings or decorative elements, emphasizing practicality over ornamentation. The proportions and construction suggest it is a medium-sized sword suitable for one-handed use.
description: A straight-edged, broad-bladed sword with a gold crossguard and a simple, dark cylindrical handle. The design is functional and unadorned, emphasizing utility.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the blade, gold crossguard, and dark handle with a faceted pommel.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the thinness of the blade and the rectangular shape of the crossguard.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, displaying the blade, crossguard, and handle from the other side.
description_view_3: Edge-on view showing the blade's narrow profile and the alignment of the handle and crossguard.
attribute_handle_design: straight cylindrical handle, dark color, simple faceted pommel, no visible grip texture
